name: session_shell
description: Persistent shell — all calls share one long-lived bash. `cd`, `export`, aliases, functions persist across calls. Use when setup and subsequent commands must share environment (activate a venv then run commands, cd into a subdir then run git, set an env var then run a script). One command at a time; concurrent calls within the session queue on the shell's stdin. Not for background processes — use `bash(run_in_background=true)` for those. Set `reset=true` to kill and restart the shell, clearing all state.

command.description: The shell command to run inside the persistent bash. `cd`, `export`, aliases, and functions persist to subsequent calls.

timeout.description: Per-command timeout in seconds (default 60). On timeout the shell is interrupted (SIGINT) and, if unresponsive, restarted; caller sees `[timed out after Ns, shell restarted]`.

reset.description: If true, kill and restart the persistent shell before running anything, clearing cwd, env, aliases, and functions. Default false.
